customer_anxiety_profile: Price-shopping, non-committal. Hiram is comparing multiple companies and has explicitly stated he will go with whoever offers the best deal. He is not motivated by pain-relief framing because the rep never connected his bill spike to Ion's solution — his decision frame is purely price.
unresolved_concerns: Competitor 'free panels' claim — Hiram heard from another company that panels are free with no payment required; this is unresolved and likely to surface early
Whether he still has to stay connected to ComEd after going solar — he asked directly at 22:21 and received a partial answer
General confusion about how solar pricing works — he noted that 'another company says a different story'
sensitive_topics_raised: Hiram recently converted his entire home to electric (stove, dryer, AC, furnace) — his bill tripled as a result; this is the core pain driver
He has a second competitor visit already scheduled in 'a couple days'
exact_promises_rep_made: Specialist will stop by tomorrow at 2PM
One hour blocked for the appointment
Specialist will show cost, incentives, and monthly savings
Specialist will explain how Hiram can make money from ComEd through net metering
closer_should_know: Hiram raised a competitor's 'free panels' claim that is unresolved — he may surface this early in the conversation. The closer should be aware this is an open question in Hiram's mind. Hiram's bill tripled after going all-electric (his words: $79 before, $209-$224 after) — this pain was never emotionally anchored by the setter, so the closer has a clean opportunity to be the first person who actually connects that number to what solar would do for him.
